name: judicial_technology_emergency_exemption_mesa_jus_mer
premise: A Judicial Technology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving judicial technology emergency exemption mesa jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Mei Navarro
agents.identity: I am Mei Navarro, accountable for a defensible call in Judicial Technology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Mateo Haddad
agents.identity: I am Mateo Haddad, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Judicial Technology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Mei Navarro must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Mei Navarro do?
